research_basis: SLSA artifact verification requires checking provenance against expectations.
Pathlib concrete paths provide filesystem-path operations appropriate for the platform.
Path traversal defenses require validating and constraining paths to intended roots.
rules: Implementation writes must use relative paths under declared allowed roots.
Absolute paths, parent traversal, home traversal, null bytes, and double slash patterns are blocked.
Write policy must require probe, parent creation, atomic write, and receipt.
Every implementation target declares operation, artifact type, hash mode, and owner.
Provenance must record builder, inputs, expected outputs, and verification expectations.
